Code fragment: public final TableField<CustomerRecord, Integer> ID = createField("id", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is named just 'id', which is a generic name that doesn't convey any meaning about the table it belongs to. Consider renaming it to 'customer_id' to make it more descriptive and to avoid ambiguity in queries with joins.

Code fragment: public final TableField<ProductRecord, Integer> ID = createField("id", org.jooq.impl.SQLDataType.INTEGER.nullable(false).identity(true), this, "");
Explanation: The primary key column is generically named 'id' instead of a more descriptive name like 'product_id'. Consider renaming the column to 'product_id' to make it self-documenting and avoid ambiguity in queries with joins.

Using selectFrom(CUSTOMER) fetches all columns from the CUSTOMER table, which may retrieve unnecessary data and reduce performance. Explicitly specify only the required columns using select(CUSTOMER.ID, CUSTOMER.NAME, CUSTOMER.EMAIL).from(CUSTOMER) instead.

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from both CUSTOMER and PRODUCT tables in the join, which retrieves unnecessary data. Explicitly list only the columns needed for ProductDTO, such as select(PRODUCT.ID, PRODUCT.NAME, ...).

Using select() without specifying columns fetches all columns from both joined tables, which may include unnecessary data and impact performance. Explicitly specify only the required columns in the select clause.
